subquery_len -> query_len: The new tokens to compute. (1 token for decode, new chunked tokens for chunked prefill)/.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Allow "None" or "" to be passed to CLI for string args that default to None
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fixes #4585
When attempting to run a server and set one of the str type flags that default to None (like --quantization), it throws an error:
python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server --model facebook/opt-125m --quantization None
...
api_server.py: error: argument --quantization/-q: invalid choic

vllm.title: Fix/async chat serving
vllm.author: schoennenbeck
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This fixes Issue 2683
Changes:

Added OpenAIServingChat._load_chat_template_async which waits for the tokenizer to become available.
According changes to OpenAIServingChat.__init__ to accomodate this (analogous implementation to ServingEngine.__init__.
Added test to check correct behaviour
Switched

vllm.title: [Kernel] Use flashinfer for decoding
vllm.author: LiuXiaoxuanPKU
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR is a first attempt to integrate flashinfer for the decoding phase. The PR still uses flash attention for the prefill phase for now.
Updated after discussion with @yzh119
Things need to be fixed:

 Use flashinfer's rope embedding --> Will turn off Flashinfer's rope support and use vllm's rope

vllm.title: [Speculative decoding] Support target-model logprobs
vllm.author: cadedaniel
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR allows vLLM to return correct log-probabilities of sampled tokens when speculative decoding is enabled. In addition, if the user specifies logprobs in their request, the correct top-k logprobs are returned.
The log-probabilities are expected to be equal to the log-probabilities when speculat

Installation time env vars are also centralized.

vllm.title: [Misc][Refactor] Introduce ExecuteModelData
vllm.author: comaniac
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The current execute_model() interface accepts the following arguments:
seq_group_metadata_list: List[SequenceGroupMetadata],
blocks_to_swap_in: Dict[int, int],
blocks_to_swap_out: Dict[int, int],
blocks_to_copy: Dict[int, List[int]],
num_lookahead_slots: int,
